text.title: HAProxy / Nginx Load Balancer Configuration Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Config MUST separate HTTP-redirect frontend, HTTPS frontend, and per-pool backends. Flat single-block configs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: `timeout connect|client|server|http-request|http-keep-alive|queue` MUST be set explicitly; relying on defaults is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: A single HAProxy/Nginx instance MUST NOT be the only LB in production; active-active or active-passive pair is required.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Public-facing frontends MUST implement per-IP rate limiting via stick tables (or equivalent in Nginx limit_req).
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: TCP backends serving databases MUST use protocol-specific checks (`mysql-check`, `pgsql-check`, `redis-check`), not bare TCP connect.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when managed cloud LB chosen OR LB still single-instance (apply HA methodology first).
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
